summary: Review #2 (external-review improvement cycle) of CC-0110. The diff (625ff0dc..HEAD, single commit) addresses all six code comments from external reviewer gndrmnn: (1) deleted controlplane_status_test.go (no meaningful coverage — only exercised generated DeepCopy and the shared conditions helper); (2) removed the tautological TestGroupVersion static-constant assertions from controlplane_types_test.go; (3) inlined the single-use aggregateReady wrapper into conditions.AllTrue(conds, subConditionTypes...) in controlplane_controller.go; (4) removed the AST/lexical TestNoInlineConditionTypeLiterals guard from controlplane_controller_test.go; (5) removed the AST/lexical TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllCallSites guard from instrumentation_test.go; (6) replaced hand-rolled ptrBool/ptrString helpers with k8s.io/utils/ptr.To in reconcile_korc_test.go and credential_invariant_test.go. Two .planwerk/review_patterns files and the progress/external-review JSON were updated as knowledge-base metadata. All changes were verified to build, vet, gofmt-clean, and pass tests; no dangling references remain and the production condition->metric invariants still hold despite the guard-test removals.

tests_checklist.item: Affected test packages pass fresh (go test -count=1 ./operators/c5c3/internal/controller/... ./operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/...)
No dangling references to removed symbols (aggregateReady, ptrBool, ptrString all return zero matches)

Removed tests were genuinely low-value (struct/DeepCopy, static-constant, and AST-lexical guards) — behavioral coverage (TestAggregateReady_*, TestReconcileKORC_Restricted*, TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames) is retained
ptr.To migration is complete — every former ptrBool/ptrString call site converted, no partial adoption

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing codebase pattern of using shared/library helpers (use-existing-utility-functions-instead-of-reimplementing-them)
aggregateReady inlining is behavior-preserving — conditions.AllTrue(conds, subConditionTypes...) is identical to the removed wrapper body
gofmt clean on all changed Go files; go vet clean; build succeeds
No duplicate imports introduced (conditions x1 in controlplane_controller_test.go; ptr added once per file)

security_checklist.item: Admin-password/App-Cred secrecy invariant test (credential_invariant_test.go) unchanged in behavior — only ptrBool->ptr.To swap; the workload-walk assertion and restricted:true check are intact
No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data introduced by the diff

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — removed a single-use wrapper and hand-rolled helpers rather than adding abstraction

Production condition->metric drift invariant still holds: all 5 instrumentSubReconciler call-site names (Infrastructure/Keystone/KORC/AdminCredential/Catalog) are exactly the keys of subReconcilerConditionTypes and map to the 5 subConditionTypes constants; every SetCondition call uses a conditionType* constant (no inline literals)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — reimplemented helpers (ptrBool/ptrString/aggregateReady) eliminated in favor of canonical utilities

fail_fast_checklist.item: No error-handling paths changed by the diff (test-only and a behavior-preserving inline refactor)

defensive_checklist.item: No new external-input handling; diff is test removals + helper inlining

suggested_improvements: Minor (metadata only): the CC-0110 example appended to .planwerk/review_patterns/replace-fixed-sleeps-with-condition-based-waits-in-tests.md describes removing tautological/lexical tests ('we are not testing static constants', 'lexical tests', 'adds no meaningful tests'), which is unrelated to fixed-sleep/condition-based-wait synchronization. This lesson would be more accurately filed under the existing reject-tautological-constant-tests.md or remove-tests-that-only-verify-language-semantics.md patterns. No code impact; consider relocating the example for knowledge-base accuracy.
next_steps: No blocking or major action items. All six external-review requests are correctly addressed; the branch is ready to proceed.
Optionally relocate the mis-filed CC-0110 review-pattern example noted in suggested_improvements.
